tcb_info.app_compose:
{
    "manifest_version": 2,
    "name": "dstack-0.5.4",
    "runner": "docker-compose",
    "docker_compose_file": "# Docker Compose configuration for Cloud API in a VPC environment
# This setup includes the dstack-service for VPC management and an init container
# to prepare necessary configuration files before starting the Cloud API service.

services:
  # Datadog Service Definition
  datadog-agent:
    image: datadog/agent@sha256:5556fb80b952832719a76b016f905616c76ee0989a239c4680c6220148e865d6
    container_name: datadog-agent
    environment:
      - DD_API_KEY=${DD_API_KEY}
      - DD_SITE=us3.datadoghq.com
      - DD_ENV=prod
      - DD_LOGS_ENABLED=true
      - DD_OTLP_CONFIG_LOGS_ENABLED=true
      - DD_LOGS_CONFIG_CONTAINER_COLLECT_ALL=true
      - DD_CONTAINER_EXCLUDE_LOGS=name:datadog-agent
      - DD_PROCESS_AGENT_ENABLED=true
      - DD_DOGSTATSD_NON_LOCAL_TRAFFIC=true
      - DD_HOSTNAME=$DD_HOSTNAME
      # Datadog Agent 7.61+ can fail to start its OTLP receiver in Docker
      # when it tries to collect process metrics through /host/proc.
      - HOST_PROC=/proc
      - DD_OTLP_CONFIG_RECEIVER_PROTOCOLS_GRPC_ENDPOINT=0.0.0.0:4317
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
      - /proc/:/host/proc/:ro
      - /sys/fs/cgroup/:/host/sys/fs/cgroup:ro
      - /var/lib/docker/containers:/var/lib/docker/containers:ro
      - /run/log/journal:/run/log/journal:ro
      - /run/systemd/:/host/run/systemd/:ro
    configs:
      - source: journald_config_file
        target: /etc/datadog-agent/conf.d/journald.d/conf.yaml
        mode: 0755
    restart: unless-stopped
    logging:
      driver: "local"
      options:
        max-size: "20m"
        max-file: "3"

  # OpenTelemetry collector sidecar for in-CVM logs/metrics/traces.
  otelcol-contrib:
    image: otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib@sha256:85ac41c2db88d0df9bd6145e608a3cb023f5d8443868adbfbbf66efb51087917
    container_name: otelcol-contrib
    command: ["--config=/etc/otelcol-contrib/config.yaml"]
    user: "0:0"
    mem_limit: "512m"
    environment:
      - MONITORING_INGEST_TOKEN=${MONITORING_INGEST_TOKEN}
      - CVM_NAME=${CVM_NAME}
      - CVM_HOST=${CVM_HOST}
      - DD_HOSTNAME=${DD_HOSTNAME}
      - ENV=${ENV}
    expose:
      - "4317"
      - "4318"
    volumes:
      - /var/lib/docker/containers:/var/lib/docker/containers:ro
      - otelcol-storage:/var/lib/otelcol-contrib
    configs:
      - source: otelcol_cvm_config
        target: /etc/otelcol-contrib/config.yaml
        mode: 0444
    # Phase 4 double-ships through Datadog. When Datadog is retired, remove
    # this dependency together with the otlp/datadog_agent exporter below.
    depends_on:
      - datadog-agent
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Avoid the collector tailing its own Docker JSON log and creating a
    # feedback loop. The host-side Phase 1 collector still captures VM
    # serial/stderr if this sidecar itself needs debugging.
    logging:
      driver: "local"
      options:
        max-size: "20m"
        max-file: "3"


  # VPC Services Definition
  dstack-vpc-client:
    image: nearaidev/dstack-vpc-client@sha256:6f442f6cc1fcc10e925059df395533fedff8e5b0816c477cce78321d4a1f92d7
    container_name: dstack-vpc-client
    restart: on-failure
    cap_add:
      - NET_ADMIN
      - SYS_MODULE
    devices:
      - /dev/net/tun:/dev/net/tun
    network_mode: host
    volumes:
      - vpc_shared:/shared
      - vpc_node_data:/var/lib/tailscale
      - /var/run:/var/run
      - /dstack:/dstack
    environment:
      - NODE_NAME=cloud-api-prod
      - TUN_DEV_NAME=tailscale1
      - VPC_SERVER_APP_ID=e78c12915ad57900317b97bd16f59ae13f86f148
      - DSTACK_MESH_URL=http://localhost:9090
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "tailscale", "status"]

  dstack-service-mesh:
    image: nearaidev/dstack-vpc@sha256:f04927ee68259f992fca9bb67618ac0f9c69faa314be08c31c840749691454e0
    container_name: dstack-service-mesh
    restart: on-failure
    ports:
      - "443:443"
      - "9090:80"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/dstack.sock:/var/run/dstack.sock
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - vpc_shared:/vpc/0:ro
    privileged: true
    environment:
      - DSTACK_GATEWAY_DOMAIN=cvm1.near.ai
      - DSTACK_MESH_BACKEND=127.0.0.1:8000
      - DSTACK_CONTAINER_IMAGE_ID=nearaidev/dstack-vpc@sha256:f04927ee68259f992fca9bb67618ac0f9c69faa314be08c31c840749691454e0
      - RUST_LOG=error
    command: /scripts/mesh-serve.sh


  # Init container to prepare configuration
  api-init:
    image: alpine@sha256:4b7ce07002c69e8f3d704a9c5d6fd3053be500b7f1c69fc0d80990c2ad8dd412
    container_name: api-init
    restart: "no"
    volumes:
      - api_config:/config
      - vpc_shared:/shared
    configs:
      - source: api_init_script
        target: /init.sh
        mode: 0755
    command: ["/init.sh"]

  # Cloud API service
  cloud-api:
    container_name: cloud-api
    image: nearaidev/cloud-api@sha256:a6a440f37641f51d44731f7ddbe4bdde3520aa43bf03eb266d03c9b9e975b9b7
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - /var/run/dstack.sock:/var/run/dstack.sock
      - api_config:/config:ro
      - ingress_certs:/ingress-certs:ro
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_PRIMARY_APP_ID=c5f76292a3df94d50056b08e57fc30fe1081ad40
      - VPC_SERVER_APP_ID=e78c12915ad57900317b97bd16f59ae13f86f148
      - SERVER_HOST=0.0.0.0
      - SERVER_PORT=3000
      - DATABASE_MAX_CONNECTIONS=32
      - DATABASE_REFRESH_INTERVAL=300
      - MODEL_DISCOVERY_SERVER_URL=${MODEL_DISCOVERY_SERVER_URL}
      - MODEL_DISCOVERY_API_KEY=${MODEL_DISCOVERY_API_KEY}
      # Shared service-token for POST /v1/internal/usage. Forwarded
      # from the CVM .env file (written by Ansible from the host's
      # CLOUD_API_USAGE_TOKEN env var, in turn from the GH Actions
      # secret of the same name). When the .env doesn't define the
      # var, Docker Compose substitutes "" and cloud-api's config
      # layer coerces empty to None → endpoint stays disabled (503).
      - CLOUD_API_USAGE_TOKEN=${CLOUD_API_USAGE_TOKEN}
      - MODEL_DISCOVERY_REFRESH_INTERVAL=60
      - MODEL_DISCOVERY_TIMEOUT=5
      - LOG_LEVEL=info
      - LOG_FORMAT=json
      - RUST_LOG=info
      - RUST_BACKTRACE=1
      - LOG_MODULE_API=info
      - LOG_MODULE_SERVICES=info
      - DSTACK_CLIENT_URL=http://localhost:8000
      - AUTH_MOCK=false
      - GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=${GITHUB_CLIENT_ID}
      - GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=${GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET}
      - GITHUB_REDIRECT_URL=https://cloud-api.near.ai/v1/auth/callback
      - GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID}
      - GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=${GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET}
      - GOOGLE_REDIRECT_URL=https://cloud-api.near.ai/v1/auth/callback
      - AUTH_ADMIN_DOMAINS=${AUTH_ADMIN_DOMAINS}
      - DATABASE_NAME=${DATABASE_NAME}
      - DATABASE_USERNAME=${DATABASE_USERNAME}
      - DATABASE_TLS_ENABLED=true
      - DATABASE_PASSWORD_FILE=/config/postgres-password
      - AUTH_ENCODING_KEY=${AUTH_ENCODING_KEY}
      - VPC_HOSTNAME_FILE=/config/vpc_hostname
      - VPC_SHARED_SECRET_FILE=/config/keyfile
      - S3_ENCRYPTION_KEY_FILE=/config/s3-encryption-key
      - AWS_S3_BUCKET=${AWS_S3_BUCKET}
      - AWS_S3_REGION=${AWS_S3_REGION}
      - AWS_REGION=${AWS_S3_REGION}
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}
      - BRAVE_SEARCH_PRO_API_KEY=${BRAVE_SEARCH_PRO_API_KEY}
      - BRAVE_LLM_CONTEXT_API_KEY=${BRAVE_LLM_CONTEXT_API_KEY}
      - GATEWAY_SUBDOMAIN=cvm1.near.ai
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=${CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS}
      - ENVIRONMENT=prod
      - TELEMETRY_SERVICE_NAME=cloud-api
      - TELEMETRY_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://otelcol-contrib:4317
      - GEMINI_API_KEY=${GEMINI_API_KEY}
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - EXTERNAL_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT=${EXTERNAL_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT}
      - INVITATION_EMAIL_ENABLED=${INVITATION_EMAIL_ENABLED}
      - INVITATION_EMAIL_FROM=${INVITATION_EMAIL_FROM}
      - RESEND_API_KEY=${RESEND_API_KEY}
      - CLOUD_UI_BASE_URL=${CLOUD_UI_BASE_URL}
      # Chutes attested third-party provider (nearai/cloud-api#758). Forwarded
      # from the CVM .env (set only where the vars file defines them — staging
      # for now). Missing → Compose substitutes the defaults below → cloud-api
      # keeps Chutes hard-off (ENABLE_CHUTES != "true"/"1"; empty key → None —
      # cloud-api config filters `CHUTES_API_KEY=""` to absent via
      # `.filter(|s| !s.is_empty())`, so it fails closed (provider not registered);
      # empty CHUTES_MODELS → no models). So this passthrough is a no-op for
      # prod until its vars file opts in. CHUTES_ENABLE_STREAMING defaults off;
      # opt in per-environment via the vars file (see vars/cloud_api_stg.yaml for
      # the streaming-safety analysis). PCCS is intentionally omitted (unset →
      # dcap-qvl default collateral).
      # AUDIT NOTE: this template is shared with prod (vars/cloud_api_prod.yaml).
      # Adding these four passthroughs changes prod's rendered compose and thus its
      # KMS-registered attestation measurement; the daily 02:00 UTC
      # update_cloud_api_prod.yml cron re-registers it (idempotent add-hash with an
      # on-chain pre-check) within ~24h. Behaviorally a no-op for prod — the `:-`
      # defaults resolve Chutes hard-off — but the measurement digest does change.
      - ENABLE_CHUTES=${ENABLE_CHUTES:-false}
      - CHUTES_ENABLE_STREAMING=${CHUTES_ENABLE_STREAMING:-false}
      - CHUTES_MODELS=${CHUTES_MODELS:-}
      - CHUTES_API_KEY=${CHUTES_API_KEY:-}
      - TLS_CERT_PATH=/ingress-certs/cloud-api.near.ai/fullchain.pem
    dns:
      - 100.100.100.100
      - 1.1.1.1
    dns_search:
      - dstack.internal
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      api-init:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
      ingress:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://127.0.0.1:3000/v1/model/list || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 40s
    labels:
      com.datadoghq.ad.logs: '[{"source": "cloud-api", "service": "cloud-api"}]'
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "100m"
        max-file: "10"
        labels: "com.datadoghq.ad.logs,com.docker.compose.service"


  # Ingress sidecar: nginx reverse proxy (TLS optional)
  ingress:
    container_name: ingress
    image: nearaidev/cvm-ingress@sha256:1653c23200298a823c08c66d829c633fc36919bca1e09cf577b83b01353e0891
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - DOMAIN=cloud-api.near.ai
      - BACKEND_HOST=cloud-api
      - BACKEND_PORT=3000
      - TLS_ENABLED=true
      - CERTBOT_EMAIL=${CERTBOT_EMAIL}
      - CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=${CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN}
      - S3_BUCKET=${INGRESS_S3_BUCKET}
      - S3_PREFIX=cloud-api-prod
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID}
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY}
      - AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${AWS_DEFAULT_REGION}
      - STAGING=false
    volumes:
      - /var/run/dstack.sock:/var/run/dstack.sock
      - ingress_certs:/certs
    ports:
      - "8443:8443"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /certs/cloud-api.near.ai/fullchain.pem"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 60
      start_period: 10s
    labels:
      com.datadoghq.ad.logs: '[{"source": "nginx", "service": "ingress"}]'
    logging:
      driver: "json-file"
      options:
        max-size: "100m"
        max-file: "10"
        labels: "com.datadoghq.ad.logs,com.docker.compose.service"


volumes:
  otelcol-storage:

  api_config:
  ingress_certs:
  # VPC Volumes
  vpc_shared:
  vpc_node_data:

configs:
  # Journald conf file content
  journald_config_file:
    content: |
      logs:
        - type: journald
          container_mode: true
          # TODO: Add filters to reduce log volume

  # otelcol-contrib config for guest-level CVM telemetry. Dollar signs are
# escaped as $$ so docker compose leaves OTel's ${env:...} references intact.
  otelcol_cvm_config:
    content: |
      receivers:
        otlp:
          protocols:
            grpc:
              endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4317
            http:
              endpoint: 0.0.0.0:4318

        filelog/docker_containers:
          include: [/var/lib/docker/containers/*/*-json.log]
          start_at: end
          storage: file_storage
          include_file_path: true
          operators:
            - type: json_parser
              parse_from: body
              parse_to: attributes
              timestamp:
                parse_from: attributes.time
                layout_type: gotime
                layout: '2006-01-02T15:04:05.999999999Z07:00'
              on_error: send_quiet
            - type: move
              from: attributes.log
              to: body
              if: 'attributes.log != nil'
            - type: move
              from: attributes.stream
              to: attributes["log.iostream"]
              if: 'attributes.stream != nil'
            - type: remove
              field: attributes.time
              if: 'attributes.time != nil'
            - type: regex_parser
              parse_from: attributes["log.file.path"]
              regex: '/var/lib/docker/containers/(?P<container_id>[a-f0-9]{12})[a-f0-9]*/'
              on_error: send_quiet
            # Preserve the low-cardinality Datadog autodiscovery tags operators
            # already use as log filters. High-cardinality tags like instance
            # and model_path stay inside the raw Docker label metadata.
            - type: regex_parser
              parse_from: attributes["attrs"]["com.datadoghq.ad.logs"]
              regex: 'model:(?P<model>[^",\]]+)'
              on_error: send_quiet
            - type: regex_parser
              parse_from: attributes["attrs"]["com.datadoghq.ad.logs"]
              regex: 'deployment:(?P<deployment>[^",\]]+)'
              on_error: send_quiet
            - type: regex_parser
              parse_from: attributes["attrs"]["com.datadoghq.ad.logs"]
              regex: 'ip:(?P<ip>[^",\]]+)'
              on_error: send_quiet
            - type: regex_parser
              parse_from: attributes["attrs"]["com.datadoghq.ad.logs"]
              regex: 'port:(?P<port>[^",\]]+)'
              on_error: send_quiet
            - type: add
              field: attributes["service.name"]
              value: cvm-docker

        prometheus/self:
          config:
            scrape_configs:
              - job_name: otelcol-cvm
                scrape_interval: 30s
                static_configs:
                  - targets: ['localhost:8888']

      processors:
        memory_limiter:
          check_interval: 1s
          limit_mib: 256
          spike_limit_mib: 64

        resource:
          attributes:
            - key: host.name
              value: $${env:DD_HOSTNAME}
              action: upsert
            - key: cvm.name
              value: $${env:CVM_NAME}
              action: upsert
            - key: host.machine
              value: $${env:CVM_HOST}
              action: insert
            - key: deployment.environment
              value: $${env:ENV}
              action: upsert
            - key: service.namespace
              value: near-ai
              action: insert

        transform/docker_metadata:
          error_mode: ignore
          log_statements:
            - context: log
              statements:
                - set(attributes["service.name"], attributes["attrs"]["com.docker.compose.service"]) where attributes["attrs"] != nil and attributes["attrs"]["com.docker.compose.service"] != nil
                - set(attributes["service.name"], ParseJSON(attributes["attrs"]["com.datadoghq.ad.logs"])[0]["service"]) where attributes["attrs"] != nil and attributes["attrs"]["com.datadoghq.ad.logs"] != nil
                - set(attributes["log.source"], ParseJSON(attributes["attrs"]["com.datadoghq.ad.logs"])[0]["source"]) where attributes["attrs"] != nil and attributes["attrs"]["com.datadoghq.ad.logs"] != nil
                - set(attributes["container.name"], attributes["attrs"]["com.docker.compose.service"]) where attributes["attrs"] != nil and attributes["attrs"]["com.docker.compose.service"] != nil

        groupbyattrs/docker_service:
          keys:
            - service.name
            - container.name

        batch:
          send_batch_size: 1024
          send_batch_max_size: 2048
          timeout: 5s

      exporters:
        otlphttp/gateway:
          endpoint: https://telemetry.infra.near.ai
          headers:
            Authorization: "Bearer $${env:MONITORING_INGEST_TOKEN}"
          sending_queue:
            queue_size: 128
            storage: file_storage
        otlp/datadog_agent:
          # Phase 4 double-ships app OTLP to Datadog. Remove this exporter
          # and the otelcol-contrib depends_on above in the Datadog
          # decommission phase.
          endpoint: datadog-agent:4317
          tls:
            insecure: true
          sending_queue:
            queue_size: 128
            storage: file_storage

      extensions:
        file_storage:
          directory: /var/lib/otelcol-contrib/storage
          create_directory: true

      service:
        extensions: [file_storage]
        pipelines:
          logs/cvm:
            receivers: [filelog/docker_containers]
            processors: [memory_limiter, resource, transform/docker_metadata, groupbyattrs/docker_service, batch]
            exporters: [otlphttp/gateway]
          logs/otlp:
            receivers: [otlp]
            processors: [memory_limiter, resource, batch]
            exporters: [otlphttp/gateway, otlp/datadog_agent]
          metrics/self:
            receivers: [prometheus/self]
            processors: [memory_limiter, resource, batch]
            exporters: [otlphttp/gateway]
          metrics/otlp:
            receivers: [otlp]
            processors: [memory_limiter, resource, batch]
            exporters: [otlphttp/gateway, otlp/datadog_agent]
          traces:
            receivers: [otlp]
            processors: [memory_limiter, resource, batch]
            exporters: [otlphttp/gateway, otlp/datadog_agent]
        telemetry:
          metrics:
            address: localhost:8888
          logs:
            level: info


  api_init_script:
    content: |
      #!/bin/sh
      set -e

      echo "Cloud API initialization starting..."

      # Wait for file to exist in /shared
      echo "Waiting for /shared/shared_key to exist..."
      while [ ! -f /shared/shared_key ]; do
        sleep 2
      done
      echo "/shared/shared_key exists"

      echo "Copying shared key to /config/keyfile..."
      cp /shared/shared_key /config/keyfile
      chmod 644 /config/keyfile

      # Derive PostgreSQL password from keyfile (same algorithm as Patroni)
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD=$$(head -c 32 /config/keyfile | base64 | tr -d "\n")
      echo "$$POSTGRES_PASSWORD" > /config/postgres-password
      chmod 644 /config/postgres-password

      echo "Copying actual hostname to /config/vpc_hostname..."
      cp /shared/actual_hostname /config/vpc_hostname
      chmod 644 /config/vpc_hostname

      # Derive S3 encryption key (32 bytes) from keyfile
      # Read 32 raw bytes, which are then converted to a 64-character hex string.
      S3_ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(head -c 32 /config/keyfile | xxd -p | tr -d '\n')
      echo "$$S3_ENCRYPTION_KEY" > /config/s3-encryption-key
      chmod 644 /config/s3-encryption-key

      echo "Initialization complete"
",
    "kms_enabled": true,
    "gateway_enabled": true,
    "local_key_provider_enabled": false,
    "key_provider_id": "",
    "public_logs": false,
    "public_sysinfo": true,
    "allowed_envs": [
        "DATABASE_NAME",
        "DATABASE_USERNAME",
        "MODEL_DISCOVERY_SERVER_URL",
        "MODEL_DISCOVERY_API_KEY",
        "INFERENCE_API_KEY",
        "CLOUD_API_USAGE_TOKEN",
        "AUTH_ENCODING_KEY",
        "GITHUB_CLIENT_ID",
        "GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET",
        "GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID",
        "GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET",
        "AUTH_ADMIN_DOMAINS",
        "AWS_S3_BUCKET",
        "AWS_S3_REGION",
        "AWS_REGION",
        "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID",
        "AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY",
        "BRAVE_SEARCH_PRO_API_KEY",
        "BRAVE_LLM_CONTEXT_API_KEY",
        "CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS",
        "NEAR_EXPECTED_RECIPIENT",
        "GEMINI_API_KEY",
        "OPENAI_API_KEY",
        "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",
        "EXTERNAL_PROVIDER_TIMEOUT",
        "CERTBOT_EMAIL",
        "CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN",
        "INGRESS_S3_BUCKET",
        "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION",
        "INVITATION_EMAIL_ENABLED",
        "INVITATION_EMAIL_FROM",
        "RESEND_API_KEY",
        "CLOUD_UI_BASE_URL",
        "DD_API_KEY",
        "HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN",
        "PROXY_TOKEN",
        "ENV",
        "HOST_IP",
        "DD_HOSTNAME",
        "MONITORING_INGEST_TOKEN",
        "CVM_NAME",
        "CVM_HOST"
    ],
    "no_instance_id": false,
    "secure_time": false
}

tcb_info.app_compose:
{
    "manifest_version": 2,
    "name": "dstack-nvidia-0.5.5",
    "runner": "docker-compose",
    "docker_compose_file": "services:
  # Datadog Service Definition
  datadog-agent:
    image: datadog/agent@sha256:5556fb80b952832719a76b016f905616c76ee0989a239c4680c6220148e865d6
    container_name: datadog-agent
    environment:
      - DD_API_KEY=${DD_API_KEY}
      - DD_SITE=us3.datadoghq.com
      - DD_ENV=prod
      - DD_LOGS_ENABLED=true
      - DD_LOGS_CONFIG_CONTAINER_COLLECT_ALL=true
      - DD_CONTAINER_EXCLUDE_LOGS=name:datadog-agent
      - DD_PROCESS_AGENT_ENABLED=true
      - DD_DOGSTATSD_NON_LOCAL_TRAFFIC=true
      - DD_HOSTNAME=$DD_HOSTNAME
      - DD_OTLP_CONFIG_RECEIVER_PROTOCOLS_GRPC_ENDPOINT=0.0.0.0:4317
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
      - /proc/:/host/proc/:ro
      - /sys/fs/cgroup/:/host/sys/fs/cgroup:ro
      - /var/lib/docker/containers:/var/lib/docker/containers:ro
      - /run/log/journal:/run/log/journal:ro
      - /run/systemd/:/host/run/systemd/:ro
    configs:
      - source: journald_config_file
        target: /etc/datadog-agent/conf.d/journald.d/conf.yaml
        mode: 0755
    restart: unless-stopped
    logging: 
        driver: "json-file"
        options:
          max-size: "100m"
          max-file: "10"
          labels: "com.datadoghq.ad.logs"


  certbot:
    image: certbot/dns-cloudflare@sha256:742dbd2e61c8709b930712c38958386c3cb3928e09eeb1f1e490600c127e2edb
    restart: unless-stopped
    entrypoint: "/bin/sh -c 'trap exit TERM; while :; do certbot renew; sleep 12h & wait $${!}; done;'"
    volumes:
      - certs:/etc/letsencrypt
    configs:
      - source: cloudflare_credentials
        target: /etc/cloudflare/credentials.ini
        mode: 0600
    depends_on:
      certbot-init:
        condition: service_completed_successfully

  certbot-init:
    image: certbot/dns-cloudflare@sha256:742dbd2e61c8709b930712c38958386c3cb3928e09eeb1f1e490600c127e2edb
    volumes:
      - certs:/etc/letsencrypt
    restart: on-failure
    configs:
      - source: cloudflare_credentials
        target: /etc/cloudflare/credentials.ini
        mode: 0600
    command: >
      certonly
      --dns-cloudflare
      --dns-cloudflare-credentials /etc/cloudflare/credentials.ini
      --dns-cloudflare-propagation-seconds 30
      --email ${CERTBOT_EMAIL}
      --agree-tos --no-eff-email --non-interactive
      --key-type ecdsa --elliptic-curve secp256r1
      --cert-name completions.near.ai
      -d "*.completions.near.ai"
      -d "completions.near.ai"
      -d "$HOSTNAME.hosts.near.ai"

  compose-manager:
    image: ${COMPOSE_MANAGER_IMAGE:-nearaidev/compose-manager@sha256:44aa2344d68609700074a8076ed177bb1989c7f5fd1e175a13084d512be475e9}
    container_name: compose-manager
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    # pid:host + CAP_SYS_ADMIN + CAP_SYS_PTRACE let compose-manager run
    # `nsenter -t 1 -m -u -i -n -p -- ...` into PID 1's namespaces.
    #   - SYS_PTRACE: needed to open /proc/1/ns/* (ptrace_may_access gates
    #     this — PID 1 has `dumpable=0` because it was launched with a full
    #     cap set, so even root in the same user-ns needs SYS_PTRACE).
    #   - SYS_ADMIN: needed for the setns() that follows the open.
    # Without BOTH, every nsenter call fails with "cannot open /proc/1/ns/*:
    # Permission denied" — affecting `/dstack-agent/:action` (pre-existing)
    # and `/admin/kernel/algif-blacklist` (PR #14 / CVE-2026-31431).
    #
    # NOTE on AppArmor: on a host with AppArmor enabled, docker-default also
    # blocks this regardless of caps; the workable combo there is
    # `cap_add: [SYS_ADMIN, SYS_PTRACE]` PLUS `security_opt:
    # apparmor=unconfined`. dstack-OS does NOT ship AppArmor (verified
    # 2026-05-19 — no apparmor binaries/profiles in the rootfs, no AppArmor
    # strings in bzImage), so we omit the `security_opt` line. If a future
    # dstack-OS image enables AppArmor, this template must add it.
    pid: host
    cap_add:
      - SYS_ADMIN
      - SYS_PTRACE
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - /var/run/dstack.sock:/var/run/dstack.sock
      - /dstack/.host-shared/.decrypted-env:/app/.env:ro
      - work:/app/work
    environment:
      - GITHUB_REPO=https://github.com/nearai/cvm-compose-files
      - BEARER_TOKEN=${BEARER_TOKEN}
      - WORK_DIR=/app/work
      - MIN_TAG_AGE_HOURS=2
      - ENV_FILES=/app/.env
      # Slack ops notifications (nearai/infra#141). INSTANCE_LABEL identifies
      # this host in the message ("Deployed on gpuXX"); SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL is a
      # secret sourced from the CVM decrypted env (empty = notifications off).
      - INSTANCE_LABEL=gpu04
      - SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL=${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL:-}
    labels:
      com.datadoghq.ad.logs: '[{"source": "compose-manager", "service": "compose-manager"}]'

  compose-manager-launcher:
    image: nearaidev/compose-manager-launcher@sha256:171c1cffea23625628fc11038a590173745d83e3570d855e53ab5a91279f95bf
    container_name: compose-manager-launcher
    restart: unless-stopped
    # Host network so the launcher's health check (LAUNCHER_HEALTH_URL below,
    # http://127.0.0.1:8080/version) can reach compose-manager, which publishes
    # 8080 on the guest host via `ports: 8080:8080`. On the default bridge,
    # 127.0.0.1 is the launcher's own loopback and the check can never pass —
    # every swap/rollback would be wrongly declared failed.
    network_mode: host
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      # Mounted read-only so the launcher can pass it as --env-file to docker compose,
      # allowing BEARER_TOKEN and other secrets to be substituted in the bundled
      # compose file without the launcher holding them in its own environment.
      - /dstack/.host-shared/.decrypted-env:/dstack-env:ro
      - work:/app/work
    environment:
      - COMPOSE_MANAGER_IMAGE_REPO=nearaidev/compose-manager
      - LAUNCHER_CHANNEL=${LAUNCHER_CHANNEL:-latest}
      - LAUNCHER_COMPOSE_PROJECT=dstack
      - LAUNCHER_ENV_FILE=/app/work/.env.launcher
      - LAUNCHER_BASE_ENV_FILE=/dstack-env
      - LAUNCHER_HEALTH_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8080/version
      # compose-manager does TLS/cert + S3 + git work on startup, so a container
      # recreate can take >60s to answer /version. 60s caused a genuine swap (and
      # its rollback) to be declared failed mid-startup -> false 30-min backoff.
      - LAUNCHER_HEALTH_TIMEOUT=180
      - LAUNCHER_COSIGN_IDENTITY_REGEXP=https://github.com/nearai/compose-manager/.github/workflows/.*
      - DOCKER_REGISTRY_USER=${DOCKER_REGISTRY_USER:-}
      - DOCKER_REGISTRY_TOKEN=${DOCKER_REGISTRY_TOKEN:-}
      - LAUNCHER_STATE_FILE=/app/work/launcher-state.json
    depends_on:
      - compose-manager
    labels:
      com.datadoghq.ad.logs: '[{"source": "compose-manager-launcher", "service": "compose-manager-launcher"}]'

volumes:
  work:
  # This volume can be re-used by other containers that need access to the TLS certificates.
  certs:
    name: certs

configs:
  cloudflare_credentials:
    content: |
      dns_cloudflare_api_token = ${CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN}

  # Journald conf file content
  journald_config_file:
    content: |
      logs:
        - type: journald
          container_mode: true
          # TODO: Add filters to reduce log volume
",
    "kms_enabled": true,
    "gateway_enabled": true,
    "local_key_provider_enabled": false,
    "key_provider_id": "",
    "public_logs": false,
    "public_sysinfo": true,
    "allowed_envs": [
        "BEARER_TOKEN",
        "SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL",
        "AWS_REGION",
        "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID",
        "AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY",
        "AWS_S3_BUCKET",
        "CERTBOT_EMAIL",
        "CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN",
        "DOCKER_REGISTRY_USER",
        "DOCKER_REGISTRY_TOKEN",
        "LAUNCHER_CHANNEL",
        "DD_API_KEY",
        "HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN",
        "PROXY_TOKEN",
        "ENV",
        "HOST_IP",
        "DD_HOSTNAME",
        "HOSTNAME"
    ],
    "no_instance_id": false,
    "secure_time": false,
    "pre_launch_script": "#!/bin/bash
export ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(cat /dstack/.host-shared/.appkeys.json | jq -r .k256_key)
"
}
